name: ✧˚.♡ Lady Aphrodite ♡.˚✧
children: harmonia (harmonia%202269ed6c1f7c80ca8340c52f454228d3.md), rhodos (rhodos%202269ed6c1f7c8042bb7bf19ce30caccb.md), beroe (beroe%202269ed6c1f7c80ca8f1fc0e1e7f04d1f.md), hermaphroditus (hermaphroditus%202269ed6c1f7c80dbb128d2a25229839c.md), eros (eros%202269ed6c1f7c806086e6c44b9206244a.md), deimos (deimos%202269ed6c1f7c80909906c1fc8aa606d8.md), phobos (phobos%202269ed6c1f7c800f9aa3e7fe53314ebb.md), golgos (golgos%202269ed6c1f7c8099904dda8961aa3ae3.md), himeros (himeros%202269ed6c1f7c80168fa5ea86983d955a.md), anteros (anteros%202269ed6c1f7c80b5ae4bd31980da7473.md), pothos (pothos%202269ed6c1f7c8009af83d77fd02a5031.md), eryx (eryx%202269ed6c1f7c80119bfbc457a047cd5e.md), aeneas (aeneas%202269ed6c1f7c8045864dd54a6d215aaa.md), the horae (the%20horae%202269ed6c1f7c808b9c5afe8f206afc8a.md), priapus (priapus%202269ed6c1f7c807bae5bc6786e3c9173.md)

name: uranus
relationship notes: was born from the seafoam created when uranus was cast into the ocean

name: zeus
relationship notes: considered lady aphrodite's father-in-law through her marriage to hephaestus

name: dione
relationship notes: some myths refer to dione as lady aphrodite's biological mother

name: eros
relationship notes: god of love; positive relationship

name: hermaphroditus
relationship notes: lady aphrodite's intersex child with hermes; positive relationship

name: harmonia
relationship notes: very peaceful and positive

name: ares
relationship notes: complicated passionate relationship full of desire

name: hephaestus
relationship notes: very tense and difficult unasked for marriage; unfaithful

name: hera
relationship notes: hera's jealous that aphrodite was crowned title of the fairest goddess during the judgement of paris

name: artemis
relationship notes: opposing values. artemis values independence & her virginity

name: dionysus
relationship notes: both are very hedonistic, and friendly because of it

name: poseidon
relationship notes: he respects Her for her beauty and relation to the sea; its mutual.

name: hermes
relationship notes: began with some initial rejection but is mutually understood and positive

name: rhodos
relationship notes: daughter of poseidon; respectful relationship

name: priapus
relationship notes: son of dionysys; complicated relationship due to appearance

name: deimos
relationship notes: son of Ares; positive relationship

name: phobos
relationship notes: the god of fear. relationship isnt the best

name: beroe
relationship notes: daughter of adonis, lover of poseidon

name: golgos
relationship notes: son of adonis. was the founder of the city of golgi in cyprus, which became a major center for the worship of aphrodite. brother of beroe

name: the horae
relationship notes: positive relationship, as they represent the seasons

name: himeros
relationship notes: very sweet, longing sort of relationship since he’s the god of desire

name: anteros
relationship notes: the god of requited love; reciprocally good relationship

name: pothos
relationship notes: the god of longing; good relationship, longs for his mother’s love and gets it

name: eryx
relationship notes: king of the sicillian city of eryx

name: aeneas
relationship notes: trojan hero, son of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anchises

name: helen
relationship notes: Lady Aphrodite favors & supports helen

name: paris
relationship notes: Lady Aphrodite is both protective & supportive of him

name: hippomenes
relationship notes: Lady Aphrodite helped him win his love but punished him for being accidentally ungrateful.

name: the kharites
relationship notes: very good relationship; they work together often to spread beauty/charm/love

name: the sea nymphs
relationship notes: a casually warm & friendly relationship & mutual love for the ocean

name: helios
relationship notes: he exposed Her affair with ares, causing her to resent him

name: athena
relationship notes: athena values wisdom, physical prowess, and combat skill, whereas Lady Aphrodite doesn’t (in the same ways)

name: hestia
relationship notes: hestia values purity and is vehemently chaste, making them almost perfectly opposed

name: adonis
relationship notes: her most famous mortal lover. their tragic myth involves a rivalry with persephone and his death by a wild boar, which led to the creation of the anemone flower
